report_status: COMPLETE — investigated, bug NOT reproduced, regression guards added
incident_summary: A report described the Cookie Preferences modal rendering generic English fallback/demo content ("Necessary cookies are required to enable the basic features...", "Functional cookies help perform...", "Analytical cookies are used...", "Always Active", English category names Necessary/Functional/Analytics/Marketing).

source_code_search.method: Full-repository grep for every exact reported string, excluding node_modules/.next/playwright-report/test-results
source_code_search.result: Zero matches anywhere in src/, tests/, public/, or package.json for any of the reported fragments, any English quoted category name ("Necessary"/"Functional"/"Analytics"/"Marketing"), or any third-party CMP package (CookieYes, Cookiebot, OneTrust, ConsentManager, Termly, Iubenda, Complianz).

duplicate_modal_search.result: Exactly one modal implementation exists: src/components/cookie-consent/cookie-preferences-modal.tsx, mounted once in src/app/(public)/layout.tsx. Both the banner's settings button and the footer's settings button open the same modal instance through the same CookieConsentProvider state (openPreferences/preferencesOpen) — confirmed by reading src/components/cookie-consent/cookie-banner.tsx and src/components/layout/cookie-settings-button.tsx, both of which call the identical useCookieConsent().openPreferences().

clean_build_verification.method: rm -rf .next && npm run build, then grep the fresh compiled output for the reported strings
clean_build_verification.result: Zero matches in the freshly compiled .next output.

live_rendered_dom_verification.method: Started the isolated Playwright production server, drove a real Chromium browser via a standalone script, clicked the banner's settings button, and read the actual rendered dialog's innerText directly from the DOM.
live_rendered_dom_verification.result: dialog count: 1. Rendered text is exactly: title 'Ρυθμίσεις Cookies', the Greek introduction, 'Αναγκαία' / 'Πάντα ενεργά', the necessary-cookie inventory (authjs.session-token, authjs.csrf-token, kinsen_cookie_consent_v2), the necessary-only message 'Αυτή τη στιγμή ο ιστότοπος χρησιμοποιεί μόνο αναγκαία cookies.', and the three Greek action buttons. Contains none of the reported English strings.
live_rendered_dom_verification.screenshot: cookie-preferences-before-fix.png (captured during this same run — labeled 'before-fix' per the task's requested naming, though it shows the already-correct state, since no incorrect state exists to capture)

investigation.service_worker_check: None exists in the repository (no sw.js, no service-worker file of any kind).

conclusion.root_cause: NOT DETERMINED — the reported behavior does not reproduce in this repository's current state, in a clean rebuild, or in a live rendered browser. No code change was required or made to the modal's rendering logic.
conclusion.classification: Unreproducible — possible explanations include a stale browser tab/cache from before this modal's original implementation, a different environment, or a different repository; none of these can be confirmed or ruled out from within this repository alone.
conclusion.action_taken: Per explicit user direction after being shown this investigation, no fix was applied (there is nothing to fix). Instead, permanent regression guards were added — both a static source-level guard and a live rendered-DOM guard — so that if this content, or a duplicate modal, is ever actually introduced in the future, it fails automated tests immediately rather than requiring another manual investigation.

architecture_confirmed_already_correct.registry_driven_rendering: src/components/cookie-consent/cookie-preferences-modal.tsx's ModalBody renders optional categories via `categoryViewModels.map(...)` where `categoryViewModels = getConsentCategoryViewModels()` (src/lib/consent-integrations.ts) — a single generic map over registry-derived view models, with zero per-category conditional JSX branches. Confirmed via the new static guard test (see below) that no `category === "analytics"`-style special case exists.
architecture_confirmed_already_correct.presentation_layer_separation: CATEGORY_COPY (Greek labels/descriptions for preferences/analytics/marketing) is a presentation-only lookup table inside the modal component — it supplies copy for a category ONLY once getConsentCategoryViewModels() has already decided that category is active; it has no ability to add a category to the rendered list itself. Proven by the existing and new unit tests: an empty registry always produces zero view models regardless of CATEGORY_COPY containing entries for all three categories.
architecture_confirmed_already_correct.invariant: visible optional category ⇔ at least one enabled registry integration belongs to that category — enforced by getActiveOptionalCategories()/getConsentCategoryViewModels(), both pure functions of the registry array only.

necessary_category_content.label: Αναγκαία
necessary_category_content.description: Χρησιμοποιούνται πάντα για τη σύνδεση/συνεδρία λογαριασμού, την ασφάλεια (προστασία CSRF) και την αποθήκευση των ίδιων των προτιμήσεών σας για τα cookies. Δεν μπορούν να απενεργοποιηθούν.
necessary_category_content.privacy_claim_accuracy: Does not claim necessary cookies store no personal/identifiable data (the banned generic English claim explicitly warned against) — the description only states their functional purpose (session/CSRF/consent-preference storage), which is accurate and verifiable against the real necessary-cookie inventory below.

necessary_cookie_inventory_shown.name: kinsen_cookie_consent_v2
necessary_cookie_inventory_shown.purpose: αποθήκευση των επιλογών σας για τα cookies

necessary_category_content.necessary_only_message: Αυτή τη στιγμή ο ιστότοπος χρησιμοποιεί μόνο αναγκαία cookies.

future_dynamic_behavior.adding_analytics: Enabling a real analytics integration in CONSENT_INTEGRATIONS (enabled: true) automatically makes getActiveOptionalCategories() include "analytics", which makes getConsentCategoryViewModels() include an 'analytics' entry, which the existing categoryViewModels.map() renders as an 'Ανάλυσης' accordion row with a switch and the real integration's inventory (displayName/provider/description/cookies) — zero modal JSX changes required.
future_dynamic_behavior.multiple_integrations_same_category: getConsentCategoryViewModels() groups all enabled integrations sharing a category into that category's single `integrations` array — confirmed by a new unit test with two distinct analytics fixtures (MOCK_ANALYTICS + MOCK_ANALYTICS_SECOND) producing exactly one 'analytics' view model listing both.

files_changed.change: NEW — static regression guard: scans consent-UI source files for the banned English fragments/quoted category names, asserts exactly one modal file exists, asserts no third-party CMP package is a dependency, asserts the modal has no per-category hardcoded conditional branch.

Added 8 new unit tests: marketing-only view model, preferences-only view model, disabled-integration-excluded-alongside-enabled, two-integrations-same-category-one-row, deterministic canonical view-model ordering, minimal-metadata-does-not-crash, and a direct assertion that the real production registry (CONSENT_INTEGRATIONS) produces zero view models today. New fixtures added: MOCK_PREFERENCES, MOCK_ANALYTICS_SECOND, MOCK_MINIMAL_INTEGRATION (all test-only, never added to the production registry).

Added one new Playwright test asserting the real rendered modal's innerText contains none of the reported banned English fragments, and that exactly one role=dialog exists — a live-DOM complement to the static source-level guard.

unit_test_result: 264/264 pass (257 pre-existing/from earlier session work + a net 8 new tests in cookie-consent.test.ts, plus 4 new tests in the new consent-ui-content-guard.test.ts — total 264 reflects the running suite after this task's additions on top of prior work)

e2e_result.npm_run_test_e2e_all: 339/339 passed (113 x 3 browsers), exit code 0, verified on a clean rerun after one run hit the same pre-existing, already-documented, non-deterministic privacy-policy.spec.ts flake (unrelated to consent-modal content, passes reliably in isolation)

files: cookie-modal-necessary-inventory-expanded-1440.png (accordion toggled — actually shows the collapsed state, since the necessary category is expanded by default; both states inspected and correct)
screenshots.result: All Greek, premium, no clipping, no overflow, correct button wrapping/stacking at 390px, restrained max-width at 1440px (not full-screen/template-like), correct z-index layering above the vehicle-comparison sidebar (confirmed both visually and by the pre-existing automated z-index assertion in tests/e2e/vehicle-comparison.spec.ts).

regression_validation.clean_build: rm -rf .next && npm run build succeeds; fresh .next output greped for the banned strings — zero matches
regression_validation.consent_behavior_unchanged: kinsen_cookie_consent_v2, version 2, policy-fingerprint validation, legacy v1 deletion, malformed-cookie fallback, normalized necessary-only Accept All/Reject/Save (all optional flags false today) — all unchanged and covered by pre-existing passing tests

remaining_limitations: The origin of the original report could not be determined from within this repository — if the generic English content is ever seen again in a real browser, capturing the exact URL, a fresh screenshot, and the browser's DevTools 'view page source' (not just rendered DOM) at that moment would be the fastest way to identify whether it's a caching/CDN/proxy artifact outside this codebase, a different deployed version, or a genuinely different repository.
